factoextra 2.0.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Project Overview

factoextra is an R package for extracting and visualizing results from multivariate data analyses (PCA, CA, MCA, FAMD, MFA, HMFA) and clustering methods. It provides a unified ggplot2-based interface across multiple backend packages (FactoMineR, ade4, stats, ca, ExPosition).

Build & Development Commands

# Check package (runs R CMD check)
R CMD check .

# Build package
R CMD build .

# Install from source
R CMD INSTALL .

# Generate documentation from roxygen2 comments
Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenize()'

# Run a quick load-and-test in R
Rscript -e 'devtools::load_all(); devtools::check()'

# Render README.md from README.Rmd
Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")'

There is no formal test suite (tests/ directory does not exist). Verify changes by running R CMD check . and manually testing with examples from roxygen documentation.

Architecture

Two-Layer Design: get_* (extract) + fviz_* (visualize)

The package follows a consistent two-layer pattern for each analysis type:

  1. get_<method>() — Extracts results (coordinates, cos2, contributions) from analysis objects into standardized data frames. These normalize output across different backend packages (FactoMineR, ade4, stats, etc.).
  2. fviz_<method>() — Creates ggplot2 visualizations from the extracted results.

Analysis types and their file pairs: | Method | Extractor | Visualizer | |——–|———–|————| | PCA | get_pca.R | fviz_pca.R | | CA | get_ca.R | fviz_ca.R | | MCA | get_mca.R | fviz_mca.R | | MFA | get_mfa.R | fviz_mfa.R | | HMFA | get_hmfa.R | fviz_hmfa.R | | FAMD | get_famd.R | fviz_famd.R |

Core Infrastructure Files

  • utilities.R — Central utility file. Contains .get_facto_class() which dispatches on input object class to determine analysis type. Also contains .get_supp() for supplementary point extraction, and many internal helper functions (prefixed with .).
  • eigenvalue.R — get_eig()/fviz_eig() for eigenvalue extraction/visualization, shared across all methods.
  • facto_summarize.R — facto_summarize() which subsets and summarizes results across all method types. Key intermediary used by fviz_contrib() and fviz_cos2().
  • fviz.R — Core fviz() generic scatter plot function. All fviz_<method>() functions are wrappers around this, which itself wraps ggpubr::ggscatter().

Multi-Package Compatibility

The get_* extractors handle objects from multiple packages via S3 class dispatch using inherits(): - FactoMineR: PCA, CA, MCA, MFA, HMFA, FAMD - stats: prcomp, princomp - ade4: dudi/pca, coa, acm - ca: ca, mjca - ExPosition: expoOutput/epPCA/epCA/epMCA

Clustering Module

  • eclust.R — eclust(): unified interface for enhanced clustering (k-means, PAM, hierarchical, etc.)
  • fviz_cluster.R — Visualizes any clustering result using PCA for dimension reduction when data has >2 variables
  • fviz_nbclust.R — Optimal cluster number determination (silhouette, wss, gap statistic)
  • fviz_dend.R — Enhanced dendrogram visualization using dendextend
  • fviz_silhouette.R — Silhouette plot visualization
  • hcut.R — Hierarchical clustering with tree cutting
  • hkmeans.R — Hierarchical k-means (hybrid method)
  • cluster_utilities.R — Internal helpers for clustering operations
  • dist.R — Distance computation and visualization (get_dist/fviz_dist)

File Loading Order

Source file dependencies are managed via @include roxygen tags (see Collate: in DESCRIPTION). Key dependency chain: eigenvalue.R → utilities.R → get_pca.R → fviz.R → visualization files.

Datasets

Bundled datasets in data/: decathlon2, housetasks, multishapes, poison. Documented in corresponding R files.

Git & PR Rules

  • Do NOT add “Co-Authored-By” lines in commit or merge request messages
  • Keep commit and PR messages clean with no attribution trailers

Key Conventions

  • All visualization functions return ggplot2 objects (class "gg" + "ggplot")
  • Internal functions are prefixed with . (e.g., .get_facto_class(), .get_supp())
  • The package uses @import ggplot2 globally (declared in utilities.R)
  • stringsAsFactors = TRUE is explicit in data.frame() calls for R 4.0+ compatibility
  • roxygen2 is used for documentation (RoxygenNote: 7.1.0); NAMESPACE is auto-generated
  • The print.factoextra S3 method handles printing of factoextra result objects

Contents

Developed by Alboukadel Kassambara, Fabian Mundt.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
